zh.lifeSummary: 當Ada Bertram於1223年出生在英國諾圖波藍德莫普斯時，她的父親Roger Bertram I36歲，母親Agnes de Emmely14歲。她和Simon de Vere至少有1個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1315年在英國達勒姆比特利享年92歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Ada Bertram于1223年出生在英国諾圖波藍德莫普斯时，她的父亲Roger Bertram I36岁，母亲Agnes de Emmely14岁。她和Simon de Vere至少有1个儿子和1个女儿。她去世于1315年在英国達勒姆比特利享年92岁。

nameMeanings.name: Bertram
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German, English (mainly in southeastern Scotland, of Norman origin), French, and Danish: from the ancient Germanic personal name Bertram, composed of the elements berht ‘bright, famous’ + hramn ‘raven’. The raven was the bird of Odin, king of the gods, in ancient Germanic mythology. The personal name was common in France throughout the Middle Ages (especially in the form Bertrand ), where its popularity was increased by the fame of the troubadour Bertrand de Born (c. 1140–1214). The name was taken to England by the Normans in the forms Bertran(d), Bertram, and Bartram.

variants.name: Beltrami
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Dante, Dino, Marco, Pio, Sal, Tullio.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the personal name Beltrame, from an ancient Germanic name composed of the elements berht ‘bright, famous’ + hramn ‘raven’. See also Bertram .

variants.name: Bartram
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and North German: variant of   Bertram  .

History: William Bartram, a Quaker, had a large farm near Darby, PA, when his eldest son, John, the first American botanist, was born in 1699. John conducted botanical experiments at his own farm in Kingsessing, PA, near Philadelphia.

variants.name: Bertman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Frieda, Wilhelm.
Americanized form of German Bertmann: altered form (by folk etymology) of Bertram .

variants.name: Bethke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Eldor, Ewald, Otto, Rudi, Wilhelmina.
North German: from a pet form of the ancient Germanic personal names   Bertram   or   Berthold  . See also   Beth  .

variants.name: Bertrand
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Armand, Monique, Francois, Gilles, Raoul, Emile, Herve, Jacques, Jean-Marie, Odette.

French, Walloon, and West Indian (mainly Haiti): from the French personal name Bertrand, a variant of Bertram , coined by folk etymology under the influence of the present participle endings -and, -ant.
History: This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Beltran
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Jesus, Luis, Pedro, Jorge, Mario, Ricardo, Francisco, Rafael.
Spanish (Beltrán), Catalan, and southern French: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements berht ‘bright, famous’ + hramn ‘raven’. See also Bertram .

variants.name: Beltramo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo.
Italian: from the medieval personal name Beltramo, of ancient Germanic origin (see Bertram ).

variants.name: Bert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Philibert, Pierre.
German, French, and Polish: from a short form of various compound personal names formed with ancient Germanic berht ‘bright, famous’ (see for example Bertram ).
English: from the Middle English female personal name Berte (Old French Berte, from ancient Germanic Berta ).

variants.name: Battermann
meanings: German:
possibly an occupational name for a merchant or peddler, from Bartermann.
alternatively, perhaps from a short form of the personal name Bertram + Middle High German man ‘man’.

variants.name: Bertran
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Jorge, Eduardo, Enrique, Joaquin, Ricardo, Aleida, Ana, Ana Maria, Elena, Felipe, Jeronimo.  French Andre.
French, Occitan, and Catalan: variant of   Bertram   or   Bertrand  .

nameMeanings.name: Ada
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of uncertain origin; it was not in general use before the late 18th century. It may be a Latinate variant of the biblical name Adah . However, it has also been explained as a pet form of Adele and Adelaide , Germanic female names of which the first element is adal ‘noble’. It was borne by a 7th-century abbess of Saint-Julien-des-Prés at Le Mans.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Ada Bertram was born in 1223, in Morpeth, Northumberland, England, her father, Roger Bertram I, was 36 and her mother, Agnes de Emmely, was 14. She had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Simon de Vere. She died in 1315, in Peterlee, Durham, England, at the age of 92.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ada Bertram est née en 1223, à Morpeth, Northumberland, Angleterre, son père, Roger Bertram I, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Agnes d’Emmely, avait 14 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille avec Simon de Vere. Elle est décédée en 1315, à Peterlee, Durham, Angleterre, à l’âge de 92 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Ada Bertram 1223 in Morpeth, Northumberland, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Roger Bertram I 36 und ihre Mutter Agnes de Emmely 14. Sie hatte mit Simon de Vere mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1315 im Alter von 92 Jahren in Peterlee, Durham, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Ada Bertram è nata il nel 1223 nel Morpeth, Northumberland, England, sua padre, Roger Bertram I, aveva 36 anni e sua madre, Agnes de Emmely, aveva 14 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia con Simon de Vere. Morì nel 1315, nel Peterlee, Durham, England, all'età di 92 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Ada Bertram(이)가 1223년 영국 노르스벌랜드 모르페스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Roger Bertram I(은)는 36세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes de Emmely(은)는 14세였다. 그녀는 Simon de Vere(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1315년 영국 뒤람 피털리에서 92세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ada Bertram nasceu em 1223, em Morpeth, Northumberland, Inglaterra, seu pai, Roger Bertram I, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Agnes de Emmely, tinha 14 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha com Simon de Vere. Ela faleceu em 1315, em Peterlee, Durham, Inglaterra, com 92 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ada Bertram родилась в 1223 г. в местности морпет, нортумбирланд, Англия, её отцу по имени Roger Bertram I было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes de Emmely, 14. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь от Simon de Vere. Она умерла в 1315 г. в местности питирли, дурам, Англия в возрасте 92 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Ada Bertram nació en 1223, en Morpeth, Northumberland, Inglaterra, su papá, Roger Bertram I, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Agnes de Emmely, tenía 14 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija con Simon de Vere. Murió en 1315, en Peterlee, Durham, Inglaterra, a la edad de 92 años.
